items.prompt: Decide whether the table `(2, 6), (4, 12), (7, 21)` is proportional and justify the constant of proportionality.
items.expected_evidence: match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the unit rate for 18 miles in 3 hours and explain what it means.
items.expected_evidence: construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find 15% of 80 and explain how the percent is acting as a rate per hundred.
items.expected_evidence: solve additive comparison questions based on displayed data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates solve additive comparison questions based on displayed data with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why equal differences alone do not prove a relationship is proportional.
items.expected_evidence: match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale.

items.prompt: Explain how a unit rate and a constant of proportionality describe the same multiplicative structure.
items.expected_evidence: construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set.

items.prompt: A student says a table is proportional because it increases by 3 each row. Diagnose the additive-pattern error. Focus on scale ignorance.
items.expected_evidence: match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale.

items.prompt: A student scales one quantity by 2 but the other by 3 and still claims proportionality. Identify the breakdown. Focus on label axis confusion.
items.expected_evidence: construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set.

items.prompt: A map uses 1 cm for every 5 km. How far does 7.2 cm represent, and why is the relationship proportional?
items.expected_evidence: match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A recipe uses 3 cups of flour for 2 batches. Write an equation relating flour to batches and find the amount for 5 batches.
items.expected_evidence: construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves construct a scaled bar graph from a provided table or tally set in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, determine whether 4 notebooks for $6 and 10 notebooks for $15 are proportional and justify your reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates match bar lengths to numerical values using the graph scale on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe represent simple data sets before diagnosing scaled bar graphs and numerical data, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether represent simple data sets is secure enough to support scaled bar graphs and numerical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in represent simple data sets from a current-node issue in scaled bar graphs and numerical data.

items.prompt: Probe compare and order multidigit numbers before diagnosing scaled bar graphs and numerical data, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether compare and order multidigit numbers is secure enough to support scaled bar graphs and numerical data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in compare and order multidigit numbers from a current-node issue in scaled bar graphs and numerical data.
